description: A lazy relation accessed inside a loop issues one query per row, degrading linearly and overloading the database.
body: Failure mode: code iterates a collection of models and reads a relation per item (->author->name) without eager loading, so an N-row list fires N+1 queries, fast in development with little data and crippling in production. Detection signals: query count scales with row count; the relation is accessed in a loop with no with()/load(); slow list endpoints and database load spikes. Do not access lazy relations per row in a list. Safer fallback: eager-load the relation with with()/load() (or a Doctrine fetch join), select only needed columns, chunk large sets, and add a query-count assertion proving the count is constant regardless of rows.
